note: Comprehensive 12-week back-to-fit plan for Vedant. Phase 1 (weeks 1-4): foundation. Phase 2 (weeks 5-12): build with C25K. Each day has specific prescription. Strength sessions reference routine IDs (A/B/C from exercises.json); progression is rule-engine-driven per-exercise (RPE adjustments).

1.name: Foundation
1.summary: Build daily habit. Walk + bodyweight strength. Don't get hurt. Lose 1-3 kg.

2.name: Build
2.summary: Real strength + start running (C25K). Lose 4-6 kg.

3.name: Sustain
3.summary: Lock it in. Reach healthy BMI. Then maintain.

weeks.focus: Just show up — every day. Form > intensity. Walks at conversational pace. Strength sessions are gentle introductions.

days.label: Mon
days.name: Easy walk
days.notes: Conversational pace. Outside if possible. This is the first day of the rest of your life.

days.label: Tue
days.name: Routine A — Push & Legs (gentle intro)
days.notes: Start with WALL pushups if knee pushups feel hard. Half-depth squats are fine. Form is everything today.

days.label: Wed
days.name: Easy walk
days.notes: Recover from yesterday. Same pace as Monday.

days.label: Thu
days.name: Routine B — Pull & Core (gentle intro)
days.notes: First time doing doorway rows? Test the door is closed tight. Plank from knees is honest, not weak.

days.label: Fri
days.name: Easy walk
days.notes: End-of-week walk. Notice how your body feels. Keep notes.

days.label: Sat
days.name: Routine C — Full-body finisher
days.notes: Bookend the first week strong. Move through it without rushing.

days.label: Sun
days.name: Rest day
days.notes: Gentle walk if you want, but no obligation. Sleep, water, nutrition.

weeks.focus: Same structure, slightly more confident. Aim for 1-2 reps more on each exercise vs. last week.

days.label: Tue
days.name: Routine A
days.notes: Try +1 rep per set if last week felt manageable.

days.label: Fri
days.name: Easy walk
days.notes: Add 5 minutes today.

weeks.focus: By now habit is forming. If something felt easy last week, the rule engine will have proposed +1 rep — apply it.

days.label: Mon
days.name: Brisk walk
days.notes: Pick up the pace slightly — should still be able to talk, but rather you wouldn't.

days.label: Sat
days.name: Routine C
days.notes: Push effort up a notch — RPE 7-8 territory on the last set.

weeks.focus: Final foundation week. Next week C25K starts — get a real pair of running shoes if you don't have them.

days.label: Mon
days.name: Brisk walk
days.notes: Last 5 min — pick up the pace. Get used to feeling slightly out of breath.

days.label: Sat
days.name: Routine C
days.notes: Test the upper-end of effort. RPE 8. Form first, intensity second.

days.label: Sun
days.name: Rest day
days.notes: Plan running shoes purchase + Monday's C25K Week 1 Day 1 mentally.

weeks.focus: C25K begins. Mon/Wed/Fri = run days, Tue/Thu/Sat = strength + walk. The walks during runs are LOAD MANAGEMENT — don't skip them.

days.label: Mon
days.name: C25K W1 D1 — Run 60s/Walk 90s × 8
days.notes: Run pace = could-just-barely-hold-a-conversation. SLOW. Knee twinges? Stop early — listen to joints.

days.label: Tue
days.name: Routine A + 10 min walk
days.notes: Lighter session — your legs will be a bit sore from yesterday.

days.label: Sat
days.name: Routine A — repeat (variety)
days.notes: Doing A twice this week. Routine C gets a week off — your legs need recovery.

days.label: Sun
days.name: Rest day
days.notes: Especially important this week — first running week is hard on tissues.

weeks.focus: Run intervals get 50% longer. Strength sessions continue. Don't try to make up for missed days by doubling up.
First taste of 3-minute runs. If last week felt brutal, repeat C25K Week 2 instead — that's a real option.
5 minutes of continuous running enters the picture. Real running is here.
Day 3 is the famous 20-minute continuous run. Mentally prepare. The first two days of the week build up to it.

days.label: Tue
days.name: Routine A (light)
days.notes: Easy session. Save energy for Wednesday.

days.label: Thu
days.name: Routine B (light)
days.notes: Recovery-focused. Save for Friday.

days.label: Fri
days.name: C25K W5 D3 — 20 MINUTES STRAIGHT
days.notes: THIS IS THE BIG ONE. Slow pace. Mental more than physical. You can do it.

days.label: Sat
days.name: Easy recovery walk
days.notes: Active recovery from yesterday's milestone.

days.label: Sun
days.name: Rest day
days.notes: You ran 20 minutes straight. Internalize that.

weeks.focus: Building toward 25 minutes continuous. Pattern repeats — your body has learned to recover.
Three 25-minute runs this week. You're a runner now even if it doesn't feel like it.
28 minutes per run. Same effort, slightly longer. Then the final week (13) hits 30 minutes — your 5K.

days.label: Sun
days.name: Rest day
days.notes: Next week: 30 minutes × 3. You finish C25K. Then you build whatever you want from there.
